objective: Fix prompt trust boundaries, remove hard assistant-turn truncation, fold repair instructions into the canonical prompt contract, and widen prompt fingerprint coverage.
success_criteria: Untrusted reply-target, transcript, and tool-observation content no longer appears in the system message.
Protocol repair guidance is rendered through the canonical prompt contract instead of ad hoc runtime-authored human messages.
Assistant-turn replay no longer hard-clips rebudgeted history to the last eight messages.
Prompt fingerprinting covers the reusable system contract plus lower-priority context-envelope layout.
Runtime tests, docs, and changelog reflect the new prompt boundary and observability model.
constraints: Keep the changes scoped to the prompt contract, LangGraph loop, tests, and operator docs.
Do not revert unrelated working-tree changes from the in-progress prompt rewrite.
Use the local workflow artifact validation and repository validation gates before closing the task.

out: Live provider or Discord smoke validation
Unrelated runtime prompt behavior outside the red-team findings

milestones.title: Capture intake snapshot and touched scope
notes: Work item scaffolded from the local workflow generator.

milestones.title: Implement the scoped changes
notes: Moved untrusted transcript and tool-observation blocks out of the system role and into the lower-priority context envelope.
Removed the fixed assistant-turn slice(-8) truncation and routed protocol repair state through the prompt contract.

milestones.title: Run validation and publish the completion report
notes: Validated artifacts, lint, build, full test suite, docs gate, and local doc-link checks.

progress.summary: Created the local work item scaffold.
Implemented the prompt-boundary hardening pass and updated the LangGraph repair path.
Closed the validation stack with green lint, build, tests, docs, and workflow gates.

decision_log.decision: Keep protocol-repair guidance in trusted runtime state instead of a separate runtime-authored human message.
decision_log.rationale: This preserves the single-source prompt contract while keeping the repair instruction higher priority than user data.

decision_log.decision: Use the full rebudgeted assistant/tool transcript after trimming instead of a fixed last-eight-message window.
decision_log.rationale: Message-count clipping was the remaining continuity risk for tool-calling loops.

discoveries.summary: The prompt fingerprint previously hashed only the system message and still included dynamic runtime values.
evidence: The original fingerprint helper only redacted current_time_utc and ignored the lower-priority user-context envelope entirely.

discoveries.summary: Transcript-focused tests had to move their assertions from the system block to the user-context envelope.
evidence: Transcript and reply-target tags now render in the lower-priority prompt message instead of the system message.

validation_strategy: Run focused prompt/runtime tests while editing, then rerun the full repository gates.
Update architecture docs in the same work unit because the system/user trust boundary is a documented contract.
notes: The working tree still contains unrelated prompt-rewrite changes from the broader branch and an untracked reports/ directory that was left untouched.
